patterns.pattern_name: Fatigue is multidimensional and requires subtype-specific inference

mediator.name: fatigue_phenotype
mediator.pathway: different fatigue words map to different physiological/neurobehavioral mechanisms
mediator.mechanism: sleepiness, objective fatigability, PEM, mental fatigue, weakness, anhedonia, burnout and sickness behavior have different correlates and next measurements

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Do not infer mechanism from the word fatigue alone; first phenotype the fatigue by sleepiness, PEM timing, objective fatigability, mental/cognitive fatigue, weakness, mood/anhedonia and recovery context.

hypotheses.reason: Systematic reviews support multidimensional fatigue assessment and weak alignment between subjective fatigue and objective fatigability.

evidence.summary: Fatigue subtypes can be distinguished using multidimensional assessment; subjective reports do not always align with objective fatigability; depression/anxiety/sleep strongly confound subjective fatigue ratings; PEM is a distinctive feature in ME/CFS and long COVID.

key_sources.title: Differential Characterization of Fatigue Phenotypes
key_sources.relevant_finding: Fatigue is multidimensional; PEM distinguishes ME/CFS/long COVID; subjective and objective fatigability can diverge.
key_sources.limitations: Overlap among subtypes remains common; wearable validation limited.

limitations: Phenotypes overlap and are confounded by mood/sleep/pain.
Digital/wearable fatigue metrics need more validation.
alternative_explanations: A single patient may have multiple fatigue phenotypes simultaneously.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Evidence strongly supports multidimensional fatigue subtypes with distinct correlates and measurements.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: A provisional state when fatigue is reported but subtype and likely mechanism are not yet identified.
Objective decline in motor or cognitive performance under sustained effort, distinct from subjective fatigue.

research_gaps: Mechanistic links between PEM biomarkers and trajectories in ME/CFS vs long COVID.
Digital wearable validation across fatigue phenotypes.

most_supported_patterns: Subjective fatigue and objective fatigability often diverge.
PEM is distinctive in ME/CFS/long COVID.
highest_value_patterns_for_inference: Use time course and objective tests to separate sleepiness, mental fatigue, PEM and fatigability.
patterns_requiring_caution: Anhedonia/apathy/burnout overlap with fatigue but are separable.
Wearables can augment but not replace validated assessment.
recommended_next_queries: Brain fog phenotypes and differential inference.
summary.overall_assessment: Core differential-inference layer; include in graph.
